channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Four years of war have transformed Russia, but few will speak in public against it
item.description: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.content:encoded: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Iran war latest: Hegseth says today marks heaviest bombing yet
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Why Russia is assisting Iran's military
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Nicole Grajewski, professor at Sciences Po and author of Russia and Iran, about Russia's reported support of Iran's military.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Nicole Grajewski, professor at Sciences Po and author of Russia and Iran, about Russia's reported support of Iran's military.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Photos from Iran and across the Middle East as the war enters Week 2
item.description: More than a week of the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran has dragged in global powers, upended the world's energy and transport sectors, and brought chaos to usually peaceful areas of the region.
item.content:encoded:
More than a week of the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran has dragged in global powers, upended the world's energy and transport sectors, and brought chaos to usually peaceful areas of the region.

(Image credit: Mohammad Yassine)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump gives mixed signals on Iran war. And, how Epstein built ties to scientists
item.description: President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. vowed its 'most intense day of strikes inside Iran'
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the Pentagon was giving President Trump "maximum options" and that the war will not be "endless," a day after the president gave mixed signals about progress.
item.content:encoded:
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the Pentagon was giving President Trump "maximum options" and that the war will not be "endless," a day after the president gave mixed signals about progress.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Ukrainian towns turn to drone nets to try stop deaths
item.description: Towns in eastern Ukraine are putting up netting over sidewalks and roads to stop Russian drones from killing civilians and soldiers.
item.content:encoded: Towns in eastern Ukraine are putting up netting over sidewalks and roads to stop Russian drones from killing civilians and soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: Lebanon wants talks with Israel to end the fighting
item.description: Lebanon says it's ready to talk to Israel to end the fighting, as the war in Iran intensifies.
item.content:encoded: Lebanon says it's ready to talk to Israel to end the fighting, as the war in Iran intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Australia grants asylum to 5 members of the Iranian women's soccer team
item.description: Australia has granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women's soccer team who were in the country for a tournament when the Iran war began.
item.content:encoded:
Australia has granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women's soccer team who were in the country for a tournament when the Iran war began.

(Image credit: Dave Hunt)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: As U.S. and Israel strike Iran, China's foreign policy experts question Washington
item.description: The U.S. and Israeli joint attacks on Iran have prompted alarm and intense discussion among China's foreign policy elite as they prepare for a U.S. presidential visit.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israeli joint attacks on Iran have prompted alarm and intense discussion among China's foreign policy elite as they prepare for a U.S. presidential visit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng
